name: Fortinet, Inc.
summary: Fortinet, Inc. is a global cybersecurity company headquartered in Sunnyvale, California, focused on the convergence of networking and security. Its integrated platform, the Fortinet Security Fabric, spans secure networking, unified SASE, and AI-driven security operations. Key proprietary technologies include FortiOS (unified operating system), FortiASIC (custom security processors), FortiCloud (private SaaS cloud), FortiAI (AI-powered security), and FortiEndpoint. As of December 31, 2025, Fortinet served end-customers in over 100 countries across financial services, retail, healthcare, OT, government, and service provider verticals.

products.name: FortiOS
products.description: Unified operating system enabling convergence of networking and AI-powered security across all form factors and edges.

products.name: FortiASIC
products.description: ASIC-based security processing units (SPUs) that increase speed, scale, and efficiency of Fortinet appliances.

products.name: FortiCloud
products.description: Organically built global private cloud SaaS platform powered by FortiStack for secure cloud services.

products.name: FortiAI
products.description: Dual-layered AI defense framework including FortiAI-Assist, FortiAI-Protect, and FortiAI-SecureAI across the Security Fabric.

products.name: FortiEndpoint
products.description: Single-agent solution converging secure connectivity, endpoint protection, EDR, and Universal ZTNA.

products.name: Fortinet Security Fabric
products.description: Integrated cybersecurity platform spanning secure networking, unified SASE, and AI-driven SecOps.

products.name: FortiGuard OT Security Services
products.description: Security services for operational technology, CPS assets, and critical infrastructure environments.

products.name: FortiCare
products.description: Technical support services provided to Fortinet customers.

products.name: FortiGuard
products.description: Security subscription services including threat intelligence and OT security services.

partnerships.description: Fortinet relies on third-party channel partners for substantially all of its billings and revenue.
A small number of distributors represent a large percentage of Fortinet's revenue and accounts receivable.
Fortinet depends on third-party manufacturers to build its products and supply components.
Fortinet uses public cloud providers for hosting back-end labs and cloud stacks.
Fortinet uses colocation vendors for hosting operational infrastructure.
